solution: Plastic is non-biodegradable. Hence, to protect our environment, we should not use plastic bags.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List - I with List - II :

  List - I (compound)   List - II
(effect/affected species)
a Carbon monoxide i Carcinogenic
b Sulphur dioxide ii Metabolized by pyrus plants
c Polychlorinated biphenyls iii Haemoglobin
d Oxides of Nitrogen iv Stiffness of flower buds

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution: Carbon monoxide combines with haemoglobin to form carboxyhaemoglobin. High concentration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo> </mo></math>leads to stiffness of flower buds.  Polychlorinated biphenyls, are highly toxic industrial compounds. Pyrus and Vitis can metabolise nitrogen oxide.

subject: Chemistry
q: The correct set of species responsible for the photochemical smog is:
question: The correct set of species responsible for the photochemical smog is:
solution:
Smog is derived from smoke + fog, this is most common example of air pollution, photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate also known as oxidizing smog.
Unburnt hydrocarbons + Nitric oxide
NO2g→hνNOg+Og
O2g+Og→O3g
CH4+2O3→3CH2OFormaldehyde+3H2O
So the common component of photochemical smog are Ozone, No, acrolein, PAN, formaldehyde.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of the following compounds is an example of Freon?
question: Which of the following compounds is an example of Freon?
solution: C2Cl2​F2​ is an example of freon. It can act as an aerosol propellant, refrigerant, or organic solvent because it is a gas at room temperature and a liquid when cooled or compressed. Freon is a term that was commonly used to refer to a group of chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) and hydrochlorofluorocarbon (HCFC) compounds.

subject: Chemistry
q: The possibility of photochemical smog formation is more at
question: The possibility of photochemical smog formation is more at
solution:
Photochemical smog is a mixture of pollutants that are formed when nitrogen oxides and volatile organic compounds (VOCs) react to sunlight, creating a brown haze above cities. It tends to occur more often in summer, because that is when we have the most sunlight.

Photochemical smog formation will be more at Industrial areas.

subject: Chemistry
q: The water quality of a pond was analysed and its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BOD</mi></math> was found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math>. The pond has
question: The water quality of a pond was analysed and its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>BOD</mi></math> was found to be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn></math>. The pond has
options: Water has high amount of fluoride compounds
solution:
The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water has BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas polluted water has a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

So, the pond has very clean water.

subject: Chemistry
q:
A water sample has ppm level concentration of the following metals:

Fe=0.2; Mn=5.0; Cu=3.0; Zn=5.0 .

The metal that makes the water sample unsuitable for drinking is
question:
A water sample has ppm level concentration of the following metals:

Fe=0.2; Mn=5.0; Cu=3.0; Zn=5.0 .

The metal that makes the water sample unsuitable for drinking is
solution:
The concentration of the ions for drinking water are,

Fe≤0.2 ppm

Mn≤0.05 ppm

Cu≤3.0 ppm

Zn≤5.0 ppm

Here, Mn concentration is greater than the allowed limit. So, it makes the water sample unsuitable for drinking.

subject: Chemistry
q: The group of chemicals used as pesticide is
question: The group of chemicals used as pesticide is
solution: DDT(dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) and Aldrin are two synthetic pesticides that were widely used for insect control. Pesticides are groups of chemicals used for destruction of insects, weeds, fungi, bacteria etc. They are generally called insecticides, fungicides, bactericides, herbicides and rodenticides.
